title: Handling Requests and Responses
description: Work with request methods, URLs, and set response headers.

slides.title: The Request Object – `req` 📨
Echo Request Info
slides.content: This server logs the method and URL, and responds with them:

slides.title: The Response Object – `res` 📤
slides.content:
## Sending Responses

The `res` object (http.ServerResponse) is used to send data back to the client.

Common methods:
- `res.writeHead(statusCode, headers)`: set status code and headers.
- `res.setHeader(name, value)`: set a single header.
- `res.write(chunk)`: send a chunk of the response body (can be called multiple times).
- `res.end([data])`: signal that the response is complete; optionally send last chunk.

After `res.end()`, no more writing is allowed.

slides.title: Status Codes 🔢
slides.content:
## Common HTTP Status Codes

- `200 OK`: successful request
- `201 Created`: resource created (POST)
- `301 Moved Permanently`: redirect
- `400 Bad Request`: client error
- `401 Unauthorized`: authentication required
- `403 Forbidden`: permission denied
- `404 Not Found`: resource doesn't exist
- `500 Internal Server Error`: server error

Always send an appropriate status code in `res.writeHead()` or set it via `res.statusCode`.
